hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity in the SSTR2-specific TM5/TM6 pocket, thereby increasing the selectivity margin over off-target SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective for stability but insufficient for selectivity. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are needed to disrupt off-target interactions and improve SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions 1/2/5/6/11/12 to disrupt off-target contacts while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to break conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining key pharmacophore interactions.
